mission.title: Install and Configure Docker Desktop Kubernetes
mission.description: Docker Desktop for Mac provides an integrated Docker experience on macOS, including a local Kubernetes cluster. It's ideal for developers looking to test applications in a Kubernetes environment without the overhead of managing a full cluster. However, it may not be suitable for production workloads due to resource constraints and limited scalability.

steps.title: Download Docker Desktop
steps.description:
This step installs Docker Desktop, which includes Kubernetes support.

```bash

open https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac

```

> This opens the Docker Desktop download page.

steps.title: Install Docker Desktop
steps.description:
Run the downloaded installer to install Docker Desktop on your Mac.

```bash

open ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg

cp -R /Applications/Docker.app /Applications

open /Applications/Docker.app

```

> Replace with the actual downloaded file name if different.
> Copy Docker to Applications folder.
> Launch Docker Desktop.

steps.title: Enable Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step configures Docker Desktop to run a local Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

open -a Docker

sleep 10 && osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click menu item "Preferences…" of menu "Docker"'

osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click checkbox "Enable Kubernetes"'

osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click button "Apply & Restart"'

```

> Open Docker Desktop application.
> Wait for Docker to start and open preferences.
> Enable Kubernetes in the settings.
> Apply changes and restart Docker.
commands: sleep 10 && osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click menu item "Preferences…" of menu "Docker"'
osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click checkbox "Enable Kubernetes"'
osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click button "Apply & Restart"'

steps.title: Verify Kubernetes Installation
steps.description:
Check if the Kubernetes cluster is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Docker Desktop
uninstall.description:
This step removes Docker Desktop and all associated Kubernetes resources. Data loss may occur.

```bash

osascript -e 'tell application "Docker" to quit'

rm -rf /Applications/Docker.app

rm -rf ~/Library/Containers/com.docker.docker

```

> Quit Docker Desktop.
> Remove Docker application.
> Remove Docker data and settings.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Docker Desktop
upgrade.description:
Backup your Docker settings, upgrade Docker Desktop, and verify the installation.

```bash

cp -R ~/Library/Containers/com.docker.docker ~/Library/Containers/com.docker.docker.bak

open https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac

open ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg

open /Applications/Docker.app

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup Docker settings.
> Download the latest version.
> Open the downloaded installer.
> Launch Docker Desktop.
> Verify that Kubernetes is still running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Docker Desktop may not have started Kubernetes properly.

**Fix:**
Restart Docker Desktop and check the Kubernetes settings.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl command not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl is not installed or not in the PATH.

**Fix:**
Ensure Docker Desktop is installed and running, which includes kubectl.

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources error
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Docker Desktop may not have enough allocated resources.

**Fix:**
Increase CPU and memory allocation in Docker Desktop settings.

troubleshooting.title: Kubernetes context not set
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl context may not be pointing to the Docker Desktop cluster.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl config use-context docker-desktop'.

versionNotes.changes: Improved Kubernetes integration and added support for Kubernetes v1.30.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with the status 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Mac meets the minimum system requirements for Docker Desktop.
